summary: Full parity confirmed for all 6 test books. content.opf output is byte-identical. 2 minor fixes applied.

functions.name: process_document_data
functions.notes: Logging-only branches (selection, spacing, pan_zoom, comic, auto_contrast, max_id) intentionally omitted as they don't affect EPUB output. document_data.pop() calls for known keys not needed in Go (no 'unexpected key' framework). process_content_properties not called separately in Go — document style properties extracted directly from value map. font_name_replacements handled via fontFixer in render.go. default_font_size/default_line_height not stored as they're only used for validation logging in Python.

functions.name: process_content_features
functions.notes: Feature version_info processing (L143-147) omitted — only used for check_empty validation. content_features id/kfx_id validation (L149) omitted — only logs errors. Both gaps are logging-only and don't affect output.

gaps.branch: L171-175: comic default for fixed_layout books
gaps.notes: Python sets book_type='comic' when fixed_layout and no book_type. Not needed for current test books. Would be needed for fixed-layout comics.
Not applicable to Kindle e-ink devices. Only for Kindle Scribe notebooks.
Dictionary book type — not in test set
Magazine/periodical type — not in test set
Dictionary book type — not in test set
Sample flag — not in test set
Pronunciation — not used by any test book. epub.Book struct has fields but decodedBook doesn't populate them.
Children/comic book type — not in test set
Scroll mode flag — not in test set
Comic guided view — not in test set
Children book type — not in test set
Textbook/PDF type — not in test set
Go sets FixedLayout=true for all values > 0 but doesn't distinguish pdf_backed vs pdf_backed_fixed_layout. Not needed for current test books.
Reading orders handled separately in state.go organizeFragments

fixes.fix: Added check for both '' and 'none' since Go defaults to '' when $433 absent but may set 'none' explicitly from $433=$349
Split author value by '&' and trim each part, matching Python's [a.strip() for a in value.split('&') if a]

assertions.VAL-EPUB-008: pass — OPF manifest entries match for all 6 books
assertions.VAL-EPUB-010: pass — OPF metadata entries match for all 6 books
assertions.VAL-EPUB-012: pass — Guide section entries present and correct
assertions.VAL-EPUB-018: pass — Creator refinement entries with marc:relators scheme
assertions.VAL-EPUB-019: pass — MARC namespace prefix on package element
